text: Li yoo na, ki bi ji piin ki siin mɔsant u paak, ki boou, ki siab pebitɔ tanpakii,

text: Ki bi jiin a “Siisa na yare.” Ki Yisa betib a “Tɔn, i yenn-ii pa man kii teen Siisa linba tee u yar, ŋaan mun-ii bia pa kii teen Yennu linba tee Yennu yar.”
translated: The first person said, “Siisa is a good person.” The second person replied, “No, Siisa is not a good person, but Yennu is a good person.”

ref: The servants came and asked, “Sir, didn't you scatter good seed in your field? Where did these weeds come from?”
text: Ŋanne ki u ŋaatoontunna baar ki tan boiɔ a ‘Chanbaa, diboorŋanii kuukɔɔ kaa ki a daan bur a kpaab na ni-ii? Ŋaan li teen nlee ki dinatimii tan be leŋi?’
translated: Why are you looking for me like this?  Is it because of Chanbaa, the one who stole my things?  I don't know what you want from me.

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Tɔn, Jɔɔnn toomiinba na nba din nyii leŋ ki ŋmat kun yoonba na, ki Yisa ji din piin ki boi niib maŋ buboit ki jikit saa Jɔɔnn po a “Ya daan saan Jɔɔnn peŋ muuk ni na, i daan dukii a i sa saa la bee? I daan mi a i sa la muuk nba ki wouŋ damm naee?
translated: Then, John said to him, "If you are truly a prophet, tell me what I will eat tomorrow, and I will believe in you. If you are truly a prophet, tell me what I will drink tomorrow, and I will believe you."

ref: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
text: Binba na tan sa saan ki saa la tubdatu nba kaa gbennu na, ŋaan ki yentina na-n mun saan kii sii be manfoor nba kaa gbennu ni.”
translated: If you are going to tell a lie, tell a lie that will benefit you and not harm you.

text: Li yoo Yisa din dɔɔ burgbetir maŋ poor po ki kpaak yukpaktir paak ki gɔɔnt. Ŋanne ki u poorpoweiteeb fiinɔ ki yaa a, “Chanbaa, ta yaa ti kpo na, a baka kaa ti po-oo?”
translated: Yisa said to the burgbetir, "If you are poor, then I will give you some money, but if you are not poor, then what will you do?"

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: Ki jɔɔ na ŋanin ji ki sak u lɔɔ na baruŋ na, ŋaan te ki bi jiiu ki kɔɔnɔ dansarik, a see ki u tan pa pann na, wun nyinnɔ.
translated: If you see someone who is not well, you should not laugh at them, but rather show them compassion, because you may also fall ill one day.

ref: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
text: Ŋaan toontunna na nba din tun ninbinn ki la u bija na ki u baat na, ki bi bet leeb a ‘U fadiirɔe na; ŋaant tin kpiu man, ŋaan ki faar na-n kpant ti yar.’
translated: He said to them, "If you want to go, go; but if you want to stay, stay.  I will not force you.  If you want to go, go; if you want to stay, stay."
